result.markdown:
--- title: "Model 2 — Random Forest" subtitle: "Bagged trees with ranger — a robust, low-tuning baseline" --- > **Where we are:** Second of three model documents in the > [Titanic — Tidymodels Tour](../index.qmd). It follows the *same* structure as > the [XGBoost doc](02-xgboost.qmd) — recipe → spec → workflow → tune → > finalize → evaluate → save — so skim for the differences. Features come from > the [EDA](01-eda.qmd) via the shared `make_recipe()`. ## Why a random forest? Where XGBoost grows trees *sequentially* to correct residuals, a random forest grows many *independent* deep trees on bootstrap samples and averages them. It is famously forgiving: with `trees = 1000` fixed, there are really only two knobs worth tuning (`mtry`, `min_n`), it rarely overfits badly, and it gives a strong baseline with almost no fuss. We use the fast `ranger` engine and ask it for **permutation** importance. ## Setup ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} source(here::here("R", "00_setup.R")) # tidyverse + tidymodels, seed = 42 source(here::here("R", "02_features.R")) # make_recipe(data) library(tidyverse) # dplyr, ggplot2, readr, etc. library(tidymodels) # recipes, parsnip, workflows, tune, yardstick, rsample, dials library(here) # project-root-relative paths library(vip) # variable importance plots library(ranger) # the engine ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} train <- read_csv(here("data", "raw", "train.csv")) |> mutate(Survived = factor(Survived, levels = c("0", "1"))) ``` ::: ## The recipe Identical shared recipe — that is the whole point: a fair comparison across models on the same features. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} rf_recipe <- make_recipe(train) rf_recipe ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` ── Recipe ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` ── Inputs ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Number of variables by role ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` outcome: 1 predictor: 10 id: 1 ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` ── Operations ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` • Variable mutation for: str_extract(Name, "(?<=, )[A-Za-z]+(?=\\.)"), ... ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` • Collapsing factor levels for: Title ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` • Variable mutation for: SibSp + Parch + 1L, ... ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` • Variable mutation for: str_sub(Cabin, 1, 1), ... ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` • Variables removed: Name, Ticket, Cabin ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` • K-nearest neighbor imputation for: Age ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` • Mode imputation for: Embarked ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` • Median imputation for: Fare ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` • Variable mutation for: as.character(Pclass) ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` • Novel factor level assignment for: all_nominal_predictors() ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` • Dummy variables from: all_nominal_predictors() ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` • Zero variance filter on: all_predictors() ``` ::: ::: ## The model specification `trees` is fixed at 1000 (more is rarely worth it); we tune `mtry` and `min_n`. We set `importance = "permutation"` so we can plot variable importance later. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} rf_spec <- rand_forest( mtry = tune(), min_n = tune(), trees = 1000 ) |> set_engine("ranger", importance = "permutation") |> set_mode("classification") rf_spec ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` Random Forest Model Specification (classification) Main Arguments: mtry = tune() trees = 1000 min_n = tune() Engine-Specific Arguments: importance = permutation Computational engine: ranger ``` ::: ::: ## The workflow ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} rf_wf <- workflow() |> add_recipe(rf_recipe) |> add_model(rf_spec) ``` ::: ## Tuning with 5-fold cross-validation ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} set.seed(42) folds <- vfold_cv(train, v = 5, strata = Survived) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # mtry's upper bound depends on the number of predictors AFTER the recipe runs, # so we finalize() its range against the prepped predictor set rather than # guessing. min_n we give a plain range. rf_params <- rf_wf |> extract_parameter_set_dials() |> finalize(x = make_recipe(train) |> prep() |> bake(new_data = NULL) |> select(-Survived)) set.seed(42) rf_grid <- grid_latin_hypercube(rf_params, size = 20) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stderr} ``` Warning: `grid_latin_hypercube()` was deprecated in dials 1.3.0. ℹ Please use `grid_space_filling()` instead. ``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} rf_metrics <- metric_set(roc_auc, accuracy) set.seed(42) rf_tuned <- tune_grid( rf_wf, resamples = folds, grid = rf_grid, metrics = rf_metrics, # Positive class "1" is the second factor level. control = control_grid(save_pred = TRUE, event_level = "second") ) ``` ::: ### Tuning results ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} autoplot(rf_tuned, metric = "roc_auc") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![ROC-AUC across the mtry / min_n grid (autoplot).](03-random-forest_files/figure-html/tune-plot-1.png){width=768} ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} show_best(rf_tuned, metric = "roc_auc", n = 5) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: ## Finalize and fit on the full training set ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} best_rf <- select_best(rf_tuned, metric = "roc_auc") best_rf ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ```{.r .cell-code} final_rf_wf <- finalize_workflow(rf_wf, best_rf) set.seed(42) rf_fit <- fit(final_rf_wf, data = train) ``` ::: ## Cross-validated performance ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} collect_metrics(rf_tuned) |> semi_join(best_rf, by = c("mtry", "min_n")) |> select(.metric, mean, std_err, n) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: ::: {.callout-tip} Copy the `accuracy` and `roc_auc` `mean` values into the results table on the [master document](../index.qmd). ::: ### Confusion matrix ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} rf_cv_preds <- collect_predictions(rf_tuned, parameters = best_rf) rf_cv_preds |> conf_mat(truth = Survived, estimate = .pred_class) |> autoplot(type = "heatmap") + labs(title = "Random Forest — CV confusion matrix") ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Confusion matrix from out-of-fold CV predictions.](03-random-forest_files/figure-html/confusion-1.png){width=480} ::: ::: ### Variable importance ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} rf_fit |> extract_fit_parsnip() |> vip(num_features = 15, geom = "col", aesthetics = list(fill = "forestgreen")) + labs(title = "Random Forest variable importance (permutation)") + theme_minimal() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Permutation importance — drop in accuracy when each feature is shuffled.](03-random-forest_files/figure-html/vip-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: Reassuringly, the forest leans on the same engineered features as XGBoost — `Title`, `Sex`, `Fare`, class. Different algorithm, same story from the data. ## Save the fitted workflow ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Project convention: models/_fit.rds — note the file is rf_fit.rds. saveRDS(rf_fit, file = here("models", "rf_fit.rds")) ``` ::: ## Submission Generate this model's leaderboard CSV with the [parameterized submission document](submission.qmd): ```bash quarto render docs/submission.qmd -P model:rf ``` That loads `models/rf_fit.rds`, predicts on `data/raw/test.csv`, writes `models/submission_rf.csv`, and prints the exact `kaggle competitions submit` command. See the [Kaggle setup guide](setup-kaggle.md) for authentication. ## Next steps - Finish the tour with the **[Neural Net](04-neural-net.qmd)** (needs normalized inputs). - Revisit **[XGBoost](02-xgboost.qmd)** to compare importance and CV scores. - Build the CSV with the **[Submission doc](submission.qmd)**. - Record this model's CV scores in the **[master results table](../index.qmd)**.
